request.question: Which band is from Ohio, Guided by Voices or Gravity Kills?

title: TVT Records
Superstarved
Guided by Voices
Gravity Kills (album)
Jim Marcus
Rocky Morton
Gravity Kills
Hypersonic Hyperphonic
Perversion (album)
Falling (Gravity Kills song)
sentences: TVT Records was an American record label founded by Steve Gottlieb.
Over the course of its 25-year history, the label released some 25 Gold, Platinum and Multi-platinum releases.

Its roster included Nine Inch Nails, Ja Rule, Lil Jon, Underworld, KMFDM, Gravity Kills, The KLF, The Baldwin Brothers, Sevendust, Nothingface, the Wellwater Conspiracy, Brian Jonestown Massacre, The Holloways, The Cinematics, Buck-O-Nine, DJ Hurricane, Speech and Pitbull.

Its biggest commercial successes were the triple platinum Nine Inch Nails's "Pretty Hate Machine", two double platinum releases by Lil Jon, and platinum releases by Snoop Dogg and Tha Eastsidaz, Dashboard Confessional, Default and Ying Yang Twins as well as gold releases by Sevendust, Gravity Kills and The Black Crowes and Jimmy Page.
Additionally, TVT also had success overseas, achieving a gold release in Germany and Sweden with The Connells and scored platinum and gold records in Canada with Default.
Superstarved (stylized as superstarved★ or superstarved*) is the third studio album by Gravity Kills, released March 19, 2002.
The album is described as "more riff-oriented" and "grindier".
Guided by Voices (often abbreviated as GBV) is an American indie rock band from Dayton, Ohio.
It has made frequent personnel changes but always maintained the presence of principal songwriter Robert Pollard.
Gravity Kills is the debut studio album by industrial rock band Gravity Kills.
It was released in 1996 on TVT Records.
The album sold just under 500,000 copies.

James Marcus (born March 22, 1966) is a musician, artist, political activist, and respected typographer who is best known for his work as the lead singer and founder of the Industrial band Die Warzau.

He is credited as engineer, remixer, vocalist, songwriter, percussionist, drummer, bass player, pianist, keyboardist or artist on albums by Die Warzau, GoFight, Sister Machine Gun, Björk, Pigface, Pansy Division, The Swans, Haloblack, Chris Connelly, KMFDM, Skrew, Machines of Loving Grace, Stabbing Westward, Gravity Kills, Everplastic, Screaming Rachel, Dessau, Chemlab, The Final Cut, Testify, Mindfunk, Little Louis and various other artists across many different genres.

His work can be heard on some of the most popular tracks by many of these groups, such as the Pigface's "Asphole", "Aboriginal", "Steamroller", etc., KMFDM's "Light", Sister Machine gun's "Wired", "Lung", "Nothing", etc.
Rocky Morton (born 1955) is an English director.
He was the co-creator of Max Headroom and co-director on "Super Mario Bros".
Various music videos by Tom Tom Club, Talking Heads, Gravity Kills, Orgy, George Harrison and Miles Davis are credited to Morton.
Videos include "New Frontier".
Alongside his partner, Annabel Jankel, the duo made their television debut with the original "," and its Americanized version.
The duo made their big-screen debut with the remake of the film noir classic, "D.O.A.," starring Dennis Quaid and Meg Ryan.
Gravity Kills is an American industrial rock band from St. Louis, Missouri.
Their music was described by one critic as "a blending of eerie industrial rock with a pop-infused melodic chorus and a bit of hard-core head banging."
Hypersonic Hyperphonic is the second studio album by Tinfed, released on December 3, 1996 by Re-Constriction Records.

"Sonic Boom" noted that "Tinfed is one of those bands who would fit perfectly on tour with the likes of God Lives Underwater, Stabbing Westward, Filter & Gravity Kills which means they should do rather well with the current crossover audience."
"Perversion" is a 1998 album by Industrial Rock act Gravity Kills.
"Falling" is a song by industrial rock band Gravity Kills from the album "Perversion", released by TVT Records in 1998.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Which band is from Ohio, Guided by Voices or Gravity Kills?
hop1_docs: Guided by Voices | Guided by Voices (often abbreviated as GBV) is an American indie rock band from Dayton, Ohio.  It has made frequent personnel changes but always maintained the presence of principal songwriter Robert Pollard.

Let's Go Eat the Factory | Let's Go Eat the Factory is the 16th album by Dayton, Ohio rock group Guided by Voices.  The album is the first since their 2004 dissolution, and the first to feature the band's classic lineup since 1996's "Under the Bushes Under the Stars".  The album was produced by the band and recorded at the homes of members Tobin Sprout, Mitch Mitchell, and Greg Demos.  As with previous albums, it features the band's famously lo-fi 4-track sound as well as more modern production.  It is also the first Guided by Voices album to be released under the Guided by Voices, Inc. label.
Sunfish Holy Breakfast | Sunfish Holy Breakfast is an EP by Guided by Voices, a band from Dayton, Ohio.  It was released on 19 November 1996.
Falling (Gravity Kills song) | "Falling" is a song by industrial rock band Gravity Kills from the album "Perversion", released by TVT Records in 1998.
Gravity Kills (album) | Gravity Kills is the debut studio album by industrial rock band Gravity Kills.  It was released in 1996 on TVT Records.  The album sold just under 500,000 copies.

Gravity Kills | Gravity Kills is an American industrial rock band from St. Louis, Missouri.  Their music was described by one critic as "a blending of eerie industrial rock with a pop-infused melodic chorus and a bit of hard-core head banging."

Andrei Găluț | Andrei Găluț (] ; born 2 August 1984) is a Romanian rock singer.  He won the 2007–08 season of Prima TV's "Megastar" He was the lead singer of metalcore band Goodbye to Gravity from 2010 until 2015 when he was seriously injured in the Colectiv nightclub fire which killed the other four members of the band.  He was the only member of Goodbye to Gravity to survive the fire.
response.summary_1:
Primary topic: band origins — Guided by Voices and Gravity Kills

- Guided by Voices — passage [1] states Guided by Voices "is an American indie rock band from Dayton, Ohio." (also supported by passages [2], [3]; e.g., "Dayton, Ohio rock group", "a band from Dayton, Ohio")
- Gravity Kills — passage [6] states Gravity Kills "is an American industrial rock band from St. Louis, Missouri."
- Ambiguity/missing facts: none in the supplied passages regarding each band's stated origin (no multiple origin candidates).
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives:
  1. No additional retrieval needed — supplied passages unambiguously state each band's origin.
response.hop2_query: Guided by Voices Gravity Kills which from Ohio origin evidence
hop2_docs: Guided by Voices | Guided by Voices (often abbreviated as GBV) is an American indie rock band from Dayton, Ohio.  It has made frequent personnel changes but always maintained the presence of principal songwriter Robert Pollard.
Propeller (Guided by Voices album) | Propeller is the fifth album by Dayton, Ohio indie rock group Guided by Voices.

Dipper Pines | Mason "Dipper" Pines is a fictional character and one of the main characters of the Disney XD animated series "Gravity Falls".  The character is voiced by Jason Ritter, and designed by and loosely based on the childhood of series creator, Alex Hirsch.  The character first appeared on the unreleased, unnamed pilot created by Hirsch with which he pitched the show; he later appeared on the first episode "Tourist Trapped".  Dipper, along with his twin sister Mabel Pines, stars in every episode of the show.  Dipper is the star of the Gravity Falls series of shorts titled "Dipper's Guide to the Unexplained" and also appears in the shorts "Fixin' it with Soos" and "Mabel's Guide to Life".

Motivational Jumpsuit | Motivational Jumpsuit is the 20th album by Dayton, Ohio rock group Guided by Voices.  It was released in February 2014 under their own record label "Guided by Voices Inc." It reached #18 on the Top Heatseekers chart.

Universal Truths and Cycles | Universal Truths and Cycles is the thirteenth (official) album by Dayton, Ohio indie rock group Guided by Voices.  After releasing their previous two albums on TVT Records, Guided by Voices returned to Matador Records.

Please Be Honest | Please Be Honest is the 22nd album by Dayton, Ohio rock group Guided by Voices.  It was released April 22, 2016 on Guided by Voices Inc.  Robert Pollard wrote all the music and played every instrument on the record.
Vampire on Titus | Vampire on Titus is the sixth album by indie rock group Guided by Voices, from Dayton, Ohio, United States.
response.summary_2: Guided by Voices is an American indie rock band from Dayton, Ohio. Guided by Voices has undergone frequent personnel changes but has consistently featured principal songwriter Robert Pollard.
